content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Verified the claim against current source. project select at src/cli/project.rs:686-694 calls sync_active_milestone_from_project_record, but task select at src/cli/task.rs:96-102 only updates the active project pointer. resolve_active_milestone at src/cli/milestone.rs:543-560 resolves from the saved milestone pointer before falling back to the project's milestone, so the advertised replacement is not behaviorally equivalent and can leave milestone next/run/status operating on a stale milestone. This is a real correctness defect in the deprecation guidance.

## Votes

- `fr-2-3ecddec9`: Accept
  Confirmed by reading the three cited sites. project select syncs the active milestone pointer via sync_active_milestone_from_project_record (src/cli/project.rs:692), whereas task select only calls set_active_project (src/cli/task.rs:99) with no milestone sync. Because resolve_active_milestone (src/cli/milestone.rs:547-558) honors the saved milestone pointer first and only falls back to the project's milestone, a user who follows the new deprecation notice will keep the previously active project's milestone, silently routing subsequent milestone next/run/status to the wrong milestone. The remediation — extract a shared selection helper that also calls sync_active_milestone_from_project_record and add a bead-backed integration test — is correct and low-risk.
